title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: Tel Aviv, Israel / New York (Global, hybrid)
description: Finout, a cloud cost-management SaaS backed by $85M+ in funding and trusted by brands like The New York Times and Wiz, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO. The role requires 7+ years in RevOps, Sales Ops, or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR. Experience with globally distributed teams collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ is required.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment across all dimensions: Head of RevOps role matches target perfectly, 15+ years exceeds 7+ requirement, B2B SaaS and Fintech expertise directly applicable, Salesforce admin and scaling experience proven. Tel Aviv location is ideal fit.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's impressive $85M+ funding round and immediate need for a Head of RevOps—your 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS revenue engines and deep Salesforce expertise seem like a natural fit for their global growth trajectory from the Tel Aviv HQ. Given your track record with CAC/LTV optimization and cross-functional leadership in high-growth environments, I thought this Head of Revenue Operations role reporting to the CRO could be worth a conversation.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, a leader in revenue intelligence, seeks a RevOps Manager focused on territory strategy and operations. Responsibilities include maintaining territory mapping in CRM systems like Fullcast and Salesforce, leading market expansion and rep capacity modeling, and partnering with Sales leadership and Finance to optimize territory composition. Requires 3-5+ years in Revenue Operations or Sales Strategy, deep understanding of B2B SaaS GTM models, and strong analytical skills with Excel, SQL, and BI tools.
score_reason: Exceptional match for Omri's senior RevOps expertise with territory strategy and pipeline forecasting focus. Gong's revenue intelligence platform aligns perfectly with his B2B SaaS GTM background, though the role sits slightly below his typical seniority level (Manager vs Head).
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM operations and territory optimization is core to how I've driven pipeline predictability and rep productivity. Gong's work in revenue intelligence resonates deeply with my philosophy that data-driven territory strategy is the foundation of sustainable quota attainment. I'd be interested in exploring how my background in Salesforce operations, capacity modeling, and market expansion aligns with your Territory Strategy & Operations needs.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Nooks, an AI Sales Assistant Platform backed by $70M+ from top VCs including Kleiner Perkins, seeks its first GTM Engineer. The role serves as the technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization, owning setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform. Nooks has grown ARR 4x then 3x and is loved by sales teams at companies like HubSpot and Rippling. Ideal for a technical operator who wants to build go-to-market infrastructure in a high-growth sales-tech environment.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment: GTM Engineer role directly matches Omri's RevOps expertise, AI-native platform focus, and technical infrastructure background. Company's sales-tech positioning, strong VC backing, and scale (4x/3x growth) are ideal for a senior operator building GTM systems.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building revenue infrastructure in high-growth B2B SaaS, and Nooks' GTM Engineer role is exactly the kind of technical go-to-market challenge I'm energized by. Your platform's 4x then 3x growth trajectory and focus on sales team enablement aligns perfectly with how I think about RevOps as a competitive advantage, and I'd love to explore how to scale your SDR infrastructure with your technology.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Beautiful.ai, a Series B B2B SaaS presentation software company in the AI space with $16M in funding, is hiring a GTM Engineer with a base salary of $80,000-$120,000 USD. The role involves building and optimizing go-to-market systems and automation to support revenue teams. Ideal for a technically minded operator comfortable working across the GTM tech stack at a fast-growing, remote-first AI company serving B2B customers.

ai_opener: Beautiful.ai's focus on AI-native presentation software resonates with modern GTM operators—I've spent 15+ years building revenue systems for B2B SaaS companies scaling through AI-augmented processes. Your GTM Engineer role aligns directly with my experience optimizing Salesforce/HubSpot stacks, pipeline forecasting, and go-to-market automation. I'd love to explore how I can accelerate your Series B growth trajectory.

title: GTM Engineer / RevOps Builder

The role blends go-to-market engineering with revenue operations, building and optimizing the systems and automation that power the company's GTM motion. Ideal for a hands-on builder who thrives in an early-stage, fast-moving environment and wants to own the technical backbone of sales and marketing operations at a growing legal AI company.

The GTM Engineer role is exactly the kind of systems-first, automation-heavy work I thrive on. I'd love to chat about how you're thinking about ops architecture as you scale through Series A.

title: Head of Demand Generation & Growth
description: Cresta, a B2B SaaS AI company, seeks a Head of Demand Generation & Growth. Requires 7+ years of progressive B2B SaaS marketing experience including 3+ years in a growth leadership role, a proven track record owning pipeline and revenue targets, and deep expertise in paid acquisition, SEO, website optimization, and analytics. Experience with enterprise buyer journeys and ABM motions is needed, plus proficiency with Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, GA4, HubSpot, Salesforce, Clay, n8n, SEMrush, and dashboarding tools like Looker or Tableau.
score_reason: Strong alignment on B2B SaaS, AI-native company, revenue accountability, and core tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, analytics). However, this role emphasizes paid media buying and demand generation execution rather than RevOps strategy and systems architecture—shifting away from Omri's core expertise and target roles.
ai_opener: Omri here—saw Cresta is scaling AI-native GTM motion. With 15+ years building B2B SaaS revenue engines and deep hands-on experience with HubSpot/Salesforce pipeline orchestration, I'm curious how you're thinking about demand gen attribution and CAC payback in the current macro. Would be valuable to explore whether a RevOps/GTM strategy angle could complement your growth initiatives.

title: Head of Sales and Marketing (B2B SaaS)
description: saas.group seeks a Head of Sales & Marketing to lead all commercial activities for one of its brands. You'll take full ownership of the growth engine from top-of-funnel acquisition through conversion, retention, and revenue expansion, working closely with the CEO. Responsibilities include building a refreshed go-to-market strategy with positioning and ICP definition, driving multi-channel pipeline generation, implementing a metrics-driven sales motion, and owning pricing and packaging experiments. A unique chance to scale a profitable, product-led SaaS company.
score_reason: Excellent strategic fit for a senior RevOps/GTM executive—the role demands full ownership of GTM strategy, pipeline forecasting, metrics-driven sales motion, and pricing optimization, all core RevOps competencies. Remote Europe aligns with Omri's location flexibility, though the breadth (sales + marketing combined) is slightly wider than his pure RevOps sweet spot.
ai_opener: I've scaled B2B SaaS GTM engines from pipeline architecture to revenue expansion—and saas.group's mandate to rebuild go-to-market strategy with ICP refinement and metrics-driven motion is exactly the problem I solve. With 15+ years owning CAC/LTV modeling and pricing experiments in fintech and AI-native platforms, I can drive both top-of-funnel velocity and expansion revenue while working lean with your CEO.
